# Next Elite — An open source production-ready Next.js starter kit

> A production-ready, frontend-first, and highly optimized Next.js 16 + React 19 + TypeScript starter kit. Built for zero-database setups (API-first), next-intl type-safe i18n, BetterAuth permission-based RBAC, and elite developer experience (DX).

---

## 1. Project Metadata & SEO Tags

- **Project Name**: Next Elite (SaaS Boilerplate, Starter Kit, Template)
- **Framework Stack**: Next.js 16 (App Router), React 19, TypeScript 6, Node.js 22 (LTS)
- **UI & CSS System**: Tailwind CSS v4 (with custom `@theme` variables for layout chrome, spacing, and borders), shadcn/ui primitives, custom Class Variance Authority (CVA) components, view transitions
- **Performance Rating**: 100/100 Lighthouse score (Performance, Accessibility, Best Practices, SEO)
- **Primary Category**: Developer Templates / SaaS Starters / Next.js Boilerplates
- **Keywords**: Next.js 16, React 19, BetterAuth, Role-Based Access Control (RBAC), next-intl, type-safe i18n, API-driven, sitemap, robots, T3 Env, Vitest, Playwright, Tailwind v4, Next.js Boilerplate, SaaS Starter Kit, Next.js SaaS Template, Frontend-First Boilerplate, Zero-Database Starter, API-First Boilerplate, developer template

---

## 2. Core Resources

- **GitHub Repository**: [salmanshahriar/Next-Elite](https://github.com/salmanshotriar/Next-Elite) — Source code, templates, issue tracking.
- **Production Demo**: [next-elite-boilerplate.vercel.app](https://next-elite-boilerplate.vercel.app/) — Live preview site.
- **Health Check Endpoint**: `/api/health` — JSON heartbeat probe for load balancers.
- **Auth Endpoint**: `/api/auth/*` — BetterAuth API route handler.

---

## 3. Key Architectural Pillars

### A. API-First & Database-Free
Designed to connect directly to any pre-existing backend (REST/GraphQL/BFF) rather than coupling directly to a local database. The server routes check auth states, and the client handles live data caching.

```
[User] ──> [Server Component Page] ──> [requirePermission() checks BetterAuth]
                 │
                 └──> Sends Server HTML (messages from messages/ folder via next-intl)
                 │
                 └──> [Client (Browser)] ──> [useQuery / apiFetch] ──> [External Backend API]
```

### B. Feature-Based Directory Structure
Code is organized into vertical slices inside `/src/features/` instead of splitting files globally:
- `auth/` — Credentials, Google OAuth, custom RBAC permissions, and hooks.
- `theme/` — Global theme provider with pointer-coordinate water-wave animations.
- `i18n/` — Translation request configs, RTL helpers, and language selectors.
- `navigation/` — Sidebar, topbar, and mobile drawer headers.
- `site/` — Configurations from `site.config.json` used for SEO.
- `users/` — Sample module demonstrating type-safe fetch actions and custom validation hooks.
Additionally, generic layout components are placed under `/src/components/shared/` (e.g., the unified `UserDropdown` profile card).

---

## 4. Feature Specifications

### 🔑 Authentication & RBAC Guards
- **BetterAuth**: Email/password and Google OAuth supported. Admin list controlled by `AUTH_ADMIN_EMAILS` values.
- **Access Control**: Roles (`admin`, `user`) with custom `requireUser()` and `requirePermission()` server-side helpers.
- **Layout Slots**: Dashboard uses parallel routing slots (`@admin` and `@user` under `src/app/(protected)/`) rendering specific components based on the authenticated user's permissions.
- **User Profile Card**: Integrated a unified `<UserDropdown />` component across all headers (public desktop header, mobile header, admin topbar, and dashboard sidebar) which displays the user's avatar, email, and role badge inline in a single row.

### 🌐 Type-Safe i18n (next-intl)
- Supports 6 default locales: English (`en`), Bengali (`bn`), Arabic (`ar` - RTL), French (`fr`), Spanish (`es`), and Chinese (`zh`).
- Translation keys are type-checked via a global declarations overlay file (`src/global.d.ts`), causing typescript compilation to fail on invalid message keys.
- Location pathnames use cookie-based lookup (`NEXT_LOCALE`), avoiding forced URL prefixes.

### 📈 Server-First SEO & PWA
- Configured via `src/features/site/site.config.json`.
- Automatically generates OpenGraph cards, Twitter preview cards, and JSON-LD structured schemas.
- Exposes dynamic `/sitemap.ts`, `/robots.ts`, and a web application manifest `/manifest.ts` (configured at `/manifest.webmanifest`).

### 📝 Forms & Input Validations
- Driven by Zod validation schemas and React Hook Form.
- Complete form examples for login, register, and password-reset pages.
- Standard input error component (`InputError` with `zodResolver`) ensures accessibility and precise inline validation.

### ⚡ Infrastructure & Observability
- **Error Tracking**: Full Sentry instrumentation for client and server errors.
- **Server Logging**: Pino logger for structured, high-speed server execution logging.
- **Rate Limiting**: Optional rate-limiting helper configured using Upstash Redis.

---

## 5. Quick Start & Setup

### Requirements
- Node.js 22.12+ (or compatible runtimes)
- npm package manager

### Local Installation
```bash
git clone https://github.com/salmanshahriar/Next-Elite.git
cd Next-Elite
npm install
cp .env.example .env
npm run dev
```

### Seed Demo Credentials
If `NEXT_PUBLIC_DEMO_MODE=true` is enabled, the login screen provides quick-fill options:
- **User Role**: `user@test.com` (Password: `12345678`)
- **Admin Role**: `admin@test.com` (Password: `12345678`)

### Docker Deployment
```bash
# Docker Compose Run
docker compose up --build

# Standalone Docker Container
docker build -t next-elite .
docker run --rm --env-file .env -p 3000:3000 next-elite
```

---

## 6. Code Quality Gates & CI Pipeline

- `npm run check`: Evaluates type-safety (`tsc`), runs formatting checks (`prettier`), runs code analysis (`knip`), and executes tests.
- `npm run lint` / `npm run lint:fix`: Enforces formatting.
- `npm run test` / `npm run test:watch`: Runs unit specs with Vitest.
- `npm run e2e` / `npm run e2e:ui`: Performs browser test flows with Playwright.
- `.github/workflows/`: Automated gates check builds on push and pull-requests.

---

## 7. Intended Use Cases

- **Best For**: Multi-role SaaS platforms, multi-lingual web apps (with LTR/RTL demands), frontends designed to consume third-party API gateways.
- **Overkill For**: Basic single-page brochure sites, or backends tightly coupled to a local database system (API-driven design).
